extra.message: SQL injection via id parameter directly concatenated into SELECT query without sanitization. User input from params['id'] is passed directly to cursor.execute() enabling SQL injection attacks.
Path traversal vulnerability via path parameter. Although os.path.abspath is used, there is no restriction on which paths can be accessed, allowing an attacker to read any file on the system.

cwe: CWE-22: Improper Limitation of a Pathname to a Restricted Directory

extra.message: OS command injection via domain parameter. The domain parameter is directly concatenated into an nslookup command executed with shell=True, allowing arbitrary command execution.

cwe: CWE-78: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in an OS Command

extra.message: Insecure deserialization via pickle.loads() on user-supplied input from params['object']. Pickle is unsafe for untrusted data and can lead to arbitrary code execution.
SQL injection via comment parameter. User input from params['comment'] is directly interpolated into an INSERT statement using string formatting without parameterized queries.
XPath injection via name parameter. User input from params['name'] is directly embedded into an XPath expression without escaping or parameterized queries, allowing XPath injection attacks.

cwe: CWE-643: Improper Neutralization of Data within XPath Expressions

extra.message: XML External Entity (XXE) vulnerability via xml parameter. The XML parser is instantiated with load_dtd=True, resolve_entities=True, and no_network=False, which allows DTD processing and entity resolution, enabling XXE attacks.

cwe: CWE-611: Improper Restriction of XML External Entity Reference

extra.message: Remote code execution via include parameter. An arbitrary local or remote file is fetched and its contents are executed directly using exec() with user-controlled input, allowing arbitrary code execution.

cwe: CWE-94: Improper Control of Generation of Code

extra.message: SQL injection via password parameter in login query. The password parameter is directly concatenated into a SELECT statement without parameterized queries or sanitization, enabling authentication bypass.

Open redirect vulnerability via redir parameter. User input from params['redir'] is directly inserted into a meta refresh redirect without validation, allowing attackers to redirect users to arbitrary URLs.

cwe: CWE-601: URL Redirection to Untrusted Site

extra.message: Reflected Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) vulnerability via v parameter. User input from params['v'] is directly used in a regex substitution and inserted into the HTML response without encoding or sanitization.

cwe: CWE-79: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation

extra.message: JSONP callback injection via callback parameter. The callback parameter is directly interpolated into the JSONP response without validation or sanitization, allowing attackers to inject arbitrary content.

cwe: CWE-79: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation
